digest.headline: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
digest.tldr: Today's digest covers 24 significant stories across AI, developer tools, and the tech industry.

sections.title: AI & ML
sections.body: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents (TechCrunch AI). Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models (TechCrunch AI). The New Online Health Shop (Dev.to AI). AWS Launches Lambda MicroVMs for Isolated Agent and User Code Execution (InfoQ). I Wanted to Try AI Video But Kept Getting Stuck. This Tool Got Me My First Complete Video in 12 Minutes (Dev.to AI).

digest.key_takeaway: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 brings stronger agentic capabilities, lower pricing, and improved safety, positioning the model as a cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro.

articles.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
articles.summary: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 brings stronger agentic capabilities, lower pricing, and improved safety, positioning the model as a cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro.

articles.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
articles.summary: The Trump administration's erratic approach to AI policymaking has left companies across the industry with little clarity about what will govern future model releases.

articles.title: The New Online Health Shop
articles.summary:
Online Health Shop Launches a Innovative Online Shop Offering a Wide Range of Health Products

A leading name in wellness and healthy living, is excited to announce the launch of its new online shop dedicated to providing high-quality health products to customers nationwide. This new platform aims to make health and wellness more accessible and convenient by offering a diverse selection of vitamins, supplements, natural remedies, and personal care items.

The shop brings together trusted brands and carefully selected products designed to support various health needs, including immune support,

articles.title: AWS Launches Lambda MicroVMs for Isolated Agent and User Code Execution
articles.summary: AWS launched Lambda MicroVMs, a new serverless compute primitive that runs each user session or AI agent in its own Firecracker virtual machine with hardware-level isolation, snapshot-based rapid launch, and state preservation for up to eight hours. Reddit community analysis found the minimum setup costs $3.03/day, roughly 9x Fargate spot pricing. By Steef-Jan Wiggers

articles.title: I Wanted to Try AI Video But Kept Getting Stuck. This Tool Got Me My First Complete Video in 12 Minutes
articles.summary:
The Gap Between "Wanting to Try" and "Actually Doing It"


You've probably seen AI-generated videos on social media. Sora, HappyHorse, Kling — the results look amazing. You want to try it too.

But then you hit a wall:



Which model? text-to-video? image-to-video? What resolution? What parameters?

How much will it cost? API pricing is per-second. One video requires multiple model calls. What if the result sucks?

Where's the full path? Tutorials teach you to generate a 5-second clip. Then what? Script? Editing? Voiceover? Music?

Other creators make it look easy — but they clearly spent mont

articles.title: 5 Python 3.14 Features That Change How You Write Code in 2026 (And 2 I'm Still Waiting For)
articles.summary:
Python 3.14 lets you delete a line from the top of almost every file you own: from __future__ import annotations. That is one of five changes in the October 2025 release that actually reach the code you write daily. The rest: threads that use every core, strings that cannot be SQL-injected, a debugger you attach to a live process, and one dependency you can finally uninstall. I run our whole automation stack on Python, so I upgraded and kept notes on what changed in practice. Here is the short list that matters, with runnable examples.




  
  
  1. from __future__ import annotations is final

articles.title: Visual Studio Code 1.127: Everything New in the July 2026 Update
articles.summary:
If you open Visual Studio Code every day, you know the feeling. A new update appears, you skim the release notes, promise yourself you'll read them later... and then get back to debugging that one mysterious error that somehow disappeared after adding a console.log() 👀.

The Visual Studio Code 1.127 release brings several meaningful improvements, especially if you use AI-powered development, build web applications, or simply want a smoother coding experience. So what's actually worth your attention? Let's break it down. 🚀

articles.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
articles.summary: Wayve’s offering is part of a growing trend of AI startups using employee tenders as a strategic tool to attract and retain talent.

articles.title: Amazon launches new $1 billion FDE org, following OpenAI and Anthropic
articles.summary: Engineers on the new team will embed within companies to deploy purpose-built agents, focusing on fast deployments and customer self-sufficiency.

articles.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
articles.summary: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. Like Claude Code, Claude Science can autonomously carry out meaningful work when given concise, high-level instructions, and it has access…

articles.summary: Model price changes detected for DekaLLM and Inceptron. Details below.

articles.title: Step-by-Step: Setting Up Your First Affiliate Income Stream
articles.summary:
I gotta say, i built my first email list in 2019 with a free Mailchimp account and roughly 200 subscribers who were mostly friends, family, and a handful of people I met in a few Facebook groups. Four years later, that same list had grown into a real asset, and the affiliate revenue it generated was paying my rent. I'm not telling you this to brag. I'm telling you this because the gap between those two points is exactly what recurring commission programs are designed to close, and most creators I talk to have no idea how much money they're leaving on the table by promoting the wrong offers.

articles.title: Google introduces a faster, cheaper image generator with Nano Banana 2 Lite
articles.summary: Google is updating its image generator to make it faster and cheaper, making it a more useful tool for creators looking to make AI content.

articles.title: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
articles.summary: The free open source agentic program is finally invading your phone.

articles.title: X now offers an MCP server to make its platform easier for AI tools to use
articles.summary: X has launched a hosted MCP server, making it easier for developers to connect AI applications with the company’s API.

articles.title: Microsoft Brings AI-Powered Vulnerability Remediation to Azure DevOps with Copilot Autofix
articles.summary: Microsoft has announced the limited public preview of Copilot Autofix for GitHub Advanced Security for Azure DevOps, extending AI-powered vulnerability remediation to teams using Azure Repos. By Craig Risi

articles.title: Agriculture is ready for AI, but its data isn’t
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is transforming what is possible in agriculture, but industry leaders should be wary of investing in AI without first laying the groundwork.  The use cases are promising, especially for an industry navigating volatile fertilizer costs, unpredictable weather, and margins that leave little room for error. Research shows AI-enabled predictive models can improve crop…

articles.title: Context Engineering for RAG : The Four Typed Inputs Behind Every RAG Answer
articles.summary:
Enterprise Document Intelligence [Vol.1 #7bis] - Tobi Lütke and Andrej Karpathy named the practice in 2025. For a single document, each brick emits typed pieces that converge on one LLM call. Corpus, conversation, and tool extensions are follow-up work
The post Context Engineering for RAG : The Four Typed Inputs Behind Every RAG Answer appeared first on Towards Data Science.

articles.title: Java News Roundup: Hardwood 1.0, Endive 1.0, Azul Payara, Quarkus, WildFly, LangChain4j, OSSI
articles.summary: This week's Java roundup for June 22nd, 2026, features news highlighting: the GA releases of Hardwood 1.0 and Endive 1.0; the June 2026 edition of Azul Payara; point releases of Quarkus, LangChain4j; the first beta release of WildFly 41; and introducing Eliya JDK and the Open Source Sustainability Initiative (OSSI), the latter of which was founded by HeroDevs and Commonhaus Foundation. By Michael Redlich
